    /// Update an existing issue.
    #[tool(
        description = "Update an existing issue's status, priority, assignee, labels, description, design notes, or acceptance criteria. Use empty string for assignee to clear it. Labels replace existing labels when provided. Uses workspace_root if provided, otherwise uses current context."
    )]
    async fn update(
        &self,
        Parameters(params): Parameters<UpdateParams>,
    ) -> Result<CallToolResult, McpError> {
        // Convert assignee to Option<Option<String>> for clearing support:
        // - None -> None (don't update assignee)
        // - Some("") -> Some(None) (clear assignee)
        // - Some("alice") -> Some(Some("alice")) (set assignee)
        let assignee = params
            .assignee
            .map(|a| if a.is_empty() { None } else { Some(a) });

        match self
            .tools
            .update(
                &params.issue_id,
                params.title,
                params.description,
                params.status.as_deref(),
                params.priority,
                assignee,
                params.design,
                params.acceptance_criteria,
                params.notes,
                params.external_ref,
                params.labels,
                params.workspace_root.as_deref(),
            )
            .await
        {
            Ok(issue) => Ok(CallToolResult::success(vec![Content::json(issue)?])),
            Err(e) => Err(to_mcp_error(&e)),
        }
    }

    /// Close an issue.

        description = "Close (complete) an issue. Mark work as done when you've finished implementing/fixing it. Uses workspace_root if provided, otherwise uses current context."

    ) -> Result<CallToolResult, McpError> {
        match self
            .tools
            .close(

                params.reason,
                params.workspace_root.as_deref(),
            )
            .await
        {
            Ok(issue) => Ok(CallToolResult::success(vec![Content::json(issue)?])),
            Err(e) => Err(to_mcp_error(&e)),
        }
    }

impl RivetsMcpServer {
    /// Create a new rivets MCP server.
    #[must_use]
    pub fn new() -> Self {
        let context = Arc::new(RwLock::new(Context::new()));
        let tools = Arc::new(Tools::new(Arc::clone(&context)));

        Self {
            context,
            tools,
            tool_router: Self::tool_router(),
        }
    }

    /// Get a reference to the context.
    #[must_use]
    pub fn context(&self) -> &Arc<RwLock<Context>> {
        &self.context
    }
}

impl Default for RivetsMcpServer {
    fn default() -> Self {
        Self::new()
    }
}

#[tool_handler]
impl ServerHandler for RivetsMcpServer {
    fn get_info(&self) -> ServerInfo {
        ServerInfo {
            protocol_version: ProtocolVersion::V_2024_11_05,
            capabilities: ServerCapabilities::builder().enable_tools().build(),
            server_info: Implementation {
                name: "rivets-mcp".into(),
                version: env!("CARGO_PKG_VERSION").into(),
                title: None,
                icons: None,
                website_url: None,
            },
            instructions: Some(
                "Rivets MCP server for issue tracking. Call set_context first to set the workspace."
                    .into(),
            ),
        }
    }
}

/// Validate text field (description, notes, etc.)
///
/// Allows newlines but rejects control characters that could cause display issues.
/// Unlike titles, multi-line text is acceptable for descriptions and notes.
fn validate_text_field(s: &str, field_name: &str) -> Result<String, String> {
    // Check for control characters (0x00-0x1F except tab and newlines, and 0x7F-0x9F)
    if let Some(pos) = s.chars().position(|c| {
        let code = c as u32;
        // Control characters excluding tab (0x09), LF (0x0A), and CR (0x0D)
        (code < 0x20 && code != 0x09 && code != 0x0A && code != 0x0D)
            || (0x7F..=0x9F).contains(&code)
    }) {
        return Err(format!(
            "{} contains invalid control character at position {}",
            field_name, pos
        ));
    }

    Ok(s.to_string())
}

/// Validate description field
///
/// Wrapper for validate_text_field specifically for descriptions.
pub fn validate_description(s: &str) -> Result<String, String> {
    validate_text_field(s, "Description")
}

pub const MAX_LABEL_LENGTH: usize = 50;

///
/// Labels must be:
/// - 1-50 characters
/// - Lowercase alphanumeric, hyphens, and underscores only
/// - Must start and end with alphanumeric character
/// - No consecutive hyphens or underscores
///
/// Examples: `bug`, `high-priority`, `needs_review`, `v2`
pub fn validate_label(s: &str) -> Result<String, String> {
    let s = s.trim();

    if s.is_empty() {
        return Err("Label cannot be empty".to_string());
    }

    if s.len() > MAX_LABEL_LENGTH {
        return Err(format!(
            "Label cannot exceed {} characters, got {} characters",

fn validate_title_and_priority(title: &str, priority: u8) -> Result<(), String> {

        return Err("Title cannot be empty".to_string());
    }

    if trimmed.len() > MAX_TITLE_LENGTH {
        return Err(format!(
            "Title cannot exceed {} characters (got {})",

            "Priority must be in range {}-{} (got {})",
            MIN_PRIORITY, MAX_PRIORITY, priority
        ));
    }

    Ok(())
}

Acceptance Criteria Testing Prework:

1.1 THE Rivets_CLI SHALL provide a `note add <id> <text>` subcommand
  Thoughts: This is about the CLI surface existing and parsing correctly. It does not vary meaningfully with input beyond parse success. Best verified with a clap parsing example test.
  Classification: EXAMPLE
  Test Strategy: Parse `["rivets","note","add","proj-abc","hello"]` and assert the NoteAction::Add variant with the right id/text.

1.2 WHEN invoked with valid id and text THE Rivets_CLI SHALL append a Note_Entry to the Notes_Field
  Thoughts: This is general across all issues and all note text. The core is the append helper: after appending, the resulting notes must contain the new entry. This is testable across generated inputs and is subsumed by the more comprehensive separation property below.
  Classification: PROPERTY
  Test Strategy: Generate arbitrary existing-notes states and note text; assert the result contains the timestamped entry.

1.3 WHILE Notes_Field already contains content THE Rivets_CLI SHALL preserve existing content and separate with one blank line
  Thoughts: General rule over all prior notes and all new entries: result == existing + "\n\n" + entry. Highly amenable to property testing.
  Classification: PROPERTY
  Test Strategy: Generate non-empty existing notes + entry; assert result starts with existing, contains exactly one blank-line separator ("\n\n") between old and new, and ends with the entry.

1.4 WHEN Notes_Field is empty or absent THE Rivets_CLI SHALL set it to the new Note_Entry without a leading blank line
  Thoughts: Edge/boundary of the append rule (existing is None or ""). For any entry, empty existing yields exactly the entry. Combine with 1.2/1.3 into one comprehensive append property.
  Classification: PROPERTY (boundary of the append property)
  Test Strategy: existing = None and existing = "" both produce result == entry (no leading whitespace/newline).

2.1 WHEN appending THE Rivets_CLI SHALL prefix the note text with a Timestamp_Header line
  Thoughts: General over all note text: the constructed entry must begin with a timestamp header line. Testable as a property on entry structure.
  Classification: PROPERTY
  Test Strategy: For arbitrary text, build entry; assert first line matches the timestamp header pattern.

2.2 THE Rivets_CLI SHALL express the Timestamp_Header in UTC
  Thoughts: The header is produced from Utc::now(), so it is always UTC. Verifying "it is UTC" from a formatted string reduces to verifying the format carries a UTC marker and parses as a UTC datetime. Combine with 2.1/2.4 as a format property.
  Classification: PROPERTY (format structure)
  Test Strategy: Assert the header contains the UTC marker and the date/time component parses under a fixed UTC format.

2.3 THE Rivets_CLI SHALL exclude author and assignee identity from the Note_Entry
  Thoughts: This is a negative structural guarantee about what the entry does NOT contain. The entry format is defined as timestamp-header + text only; there is no code path that injects identity. Not a universal computable property (cannot assert "contains no identity" for arbitrary free text, since text could coincidentally contain a name). Best covered by an example test asserting the entry equals header + text with no extra identity fields.
  Classification: EXAMPLE
  Test Strategy: Build an entry and assert it is exactly the timestamp header line plus the supplied text, with no assignee/author segment.

2.4 THE Rivets_CLI SHALL format the Note_Entry with the Timestamp_Header on its own line followed by the note text
  Thoughts: General over all text: entry == header + "\n" + text. Combine with 2.1/2.2 into one entry-format property.
  Classification: PROPERTY
  Test Strategy: For arbitrary text, assert entry splits into header line then the exact supplied text.

3.1 WHEN update --notes is invoked THE Rivets_CLI SHALL replace the entire Notes_Field
  Thoughts: Existing behavior we must not regress. General: for any prior notes and any new value, the field becomes exactly the new value. Testable as a property.
  Classification: PROPERTY
  Test Strategy: Generate prior notes + replacement value; assert post-update notes == replacement value.

3.2 THE Rivets_CLI SHALL retain the single Option<String> structure in JSONL
  Thoughts: Structural/design constraint about the storage schema, not a runtime behavior with varying inputs. Not directly testable as a property, but the round-trip property (below) exercises that the field is a single string.
  Classification: EDGE_CASE (covered by round-trip property)
  Test Strategy: N/A directly; confirmed by serialization round-trip.

3.3 WHEN a pre-feature issue is loaded THE Rivets_CLI SHALL read its Notes_Field without error
  Thoughts: Because the schema is unchanged, any issue serialized before this feature must deserialize. This is a serialization round-trip guarantee, a classic property for a JSONL store.
  Classification: PROPERTY
  Test Strategy: For arbitrary issues (including accumulated multi-entry notes), serialize then deserialize and assert equality.

4.1 IF id does not match an existing issue THEN error and leave stored issues unchanged
  Thoughts: General over all missing ids and all prior store states: a note-add against a missing id returns an error and the stored set is byte-for-byte unchanged. Testable as a property.
  Classification: PROPERTY
  Test Strategy: Generate a store and an id not present; attempt note-add; assert Err and store unchanged.

4.2 IF note text is empty THEN error and leave Notes_Field unchanged
  Thoughts: "Empty" should include whitespace-only, mirroring how titles are trimmed. General over all whitespace-only strings: rejected with error, notes unchanged. Testable as a property.
  Classification: PROPERTY
  Test Strategy: Generate whitespace-only strings; assert validation rejects and notes field is unchanged.

5.1 THE MCP_Server SHALL expose a note-append tool
  Thoughts: Tool-surface existence; verified by a registration/smoke test, no meaningful input variation.
  Classification: SMOKE
  Test Strategy: Assert the tool is registered/listed by the server.

5.2 WHEN MCP note-append is invoked with valid id+text THE MCP_Server SHALL append using the same header format and blank-line separation as the CLI
  Thoughts: Parity property. Both paths share the same helper, so for any existing notes + text the MCP result and CLI result must be equivalent (identical modulo the timestamp instant). Model-based/confluence style property.
  Classification: PROPERTY
  Test Strategy: For arbitrary existing notes + text, assert the entry-construction used by MCP equals that used by CLI (shared helper output).

5.3 IF MCP id does not match an existing issue THEN error identifying the missing issue
  Thoughts: Specific error-path behavior; representative example is sufficient (mirrors 4.1 which is already a property).
  Classification: EXAMPLE
  Test Strategy: Invoke MCP note-append with a missing id; assert IssueNotFound-style error.
</preworkAnalysis>
</invoke>

rawInput.response:
Phase 3 (Design Generation) complete for the "multiple-notes" Quick Spec. Wrote design.md to .kiro/specs/multiple-notes/design.md based on the completed requirements.md and the actual rivets codebase.

Key design decisions (grounded in the current code):
- New `rivets note add <id> "text"` subcommand modeled on the existing `dep`/`label` subcommand pattern: `NoteArgs` + `NoteAction::Add { issue_id, text }` in cli/args.rs, a `Note(NoteArgs)` variant in the `Commands` enum, and dispatch in `Cli::execute`.
- Reuses the existing `append_note` helper (the glossary's Append_Helper, already used by close/reopen) for blank-line separation — left untouched so its "empty existing → no leading separator" contract remains the single source of truth (Reqs 1.3/1.4).
- Introduces one new pure function `build_timestamped_note(now, text)` in `rivets::domain`, producing a `[YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS UTC]` header line followed by the text. Time is injected for testability.
- Handler `execute_note_add` does fetch(get) → IssueNotFound on miss (Req 4.1, store unchanged) → build entry → `append_note` → `storage.update(IssueUpdate { notes: Some(appended), .. })` → save with reload-on-failure, matching existing conventions. Storage `update` already overwrites `notes`, so append is computed caller-side exactly like close/reopen.
- Empty/whitespace-only text rejected at parse time via new `validate_note_text` (reuses `validate_text_field`) so no storage mutation occurs (Req 4.2).
- Storage schema unchanged: `notes` stays `Option<String>` (Reqs 3.1, 3.2, 3.3).
- MCP parity: `NoteAddParams` (models.rs), `Tools::note_add` (tools.rs), and a `note_add` `#[tool]` (server.rs), reusing `build_timestamped_note` + `append_note` from the `rivets` crate. Flagged that `append_note` must be made public or (preferred) both helpers moved into a shared `rivets::domain::notes` module so CLI and MCP share one implementation (Reqs 5.1–5.3).

Included prework classification of all acceptance criteria and 7 consolidated Correctness Properties (with universal quantification and `Validates: Requirements X.Y` annotations) covering append/separation, entry format, empty-text rejection, missing-issue no-op, serialization round-trip, update overwrite, and CLI/MCP parity.

Stopped after design.md per the Fast Task workflow — did not generate tasks.md.</response>

<path>/home/dwalleck/repos/rivets/.kiro/specs/multiple-notes/design.md</path>
</file>
</files>
</invoke>

rawInput.text:
# Implementation Plan: Multiple Notes

## Overview

Implement the `rivets note add <id> "text"` subcommand and its MCP counterpart, which append a
UTC-timestamped entry to an issue's `notes` field without discarding prior content. The work
starts by extracting the note-formatting logic into a shared `rivets::domain::notes` module
(`build_timestamped_note` plus the relocated `append_note`) so both the CLI and MCP paths compose
the same helpers. It then layers CLI argument parsing, input validation, and the command handler,
followed by MCP parity. Storage stays unchanged (`notes` remains `Option<String>`), keeping the
change backward compatible. Property-based tests validate the seven correctness properties from
the design; focused example/integration tests cover parsing, error paths, and backward-compatible
loading.

## Tasks

- [ ] 1. Create shared domain note-formatting module
  - [ ] 1.1 Add `rivets::domain::notes` module and relocate the append helper
    - Create `crates/rivets/src/domain/notes.rs` with `NOTE_TIMESTAMP_FORMAT` and pure
      `build_timestamped_note(now: DateTime<Utc>, text: &str) -> String` (header line + `\n` + text)
    - Move `append_note` out of `crates/rivets/src/cli/execute.rs` into this module and make it
      `pub`; re-export both from `crate::domain` so CLI and MCP crates share one import
    - Update existing `close`/`reopen` call sites in `execute.rs` to use the relocated `append_note`
    - _Requirements: 1.2, 1.3, 1.4, 2.1, 2.2, 2.4_

  - [ ]* 1.2 Write property test for `build_timestamped_note`
    - **Property 2: Note entry is a UTC timestamp header line followed by the text**
    - **Validates: Requirements 2.1, 2.2, 2.4**
    - Assert first line matches `[YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS UTC]` and remaining content equals the input text

  - [ ]* 1.3 Write property test for `append_note` separation semantics
    - **Property 1: Append preserves prior notes and separates entries**
    - **Validates: Requirements 1.2, 1.3, 1.4**
    - Assert absent/empty prior → entry alone; non-empty prior → prior + `\n\n` + entry, prior is a prefix

- [ ] 2. Add CLI argument parsing and note-text validation
  - [ ] 2.1 Implement `validate_note_text` validator
    - Add `validate_note_text` to `crates/rivets/src/cli/validators.rs`: reject empty-after-trim,
      then delegate to existing `validate_text_field`; return the original (untrimmed) text
    - _Requirements: 4.2, 2.4_

  - [ ]* 2.2 Write property test for empty/whitespace-only note-text rejection
    - **Property 3: Empty or whitespace-only note text is rejected and leaves notes unchanged**
    - **Validates: Requirements 4.2**
    - Generate whitespace-only strings (including empty) and assert `validate_note_text` returns an error

  - [ ] 2.3 Add `NoteArgs`/`NoteAction` and `Commands::Note` variant
    - Add `NoteArgs` and `NoteAction::Add { issue_id, text }` to `crates/rivets/src/cli/args.rs`,
      wiring `validate_issue_id` and `validate_note_text`, with `allow_hyphen_values` on `text`
    - Add the `Note(NoteArgs)` variant (with doc comment) to the `Commands` enum in
      `crates/rivets/src/cli/mod.rs`
    - _Requirements: 1.1_

  - [ ]* 2.4 Write example test for `note add` clap parsing
    - Assert `note add <id> "text"` parses into `NoteAction::Add` and that a leading `-` in text is accepted
    - _Requirements: 1.1_

- [ ] 3. Implement the CLI command handler
  - [ ] 3.1 Implement `execute_note`/`execute_note_add` and dispatch
    - Add `execute_note` (dispatches on `NoteAction`) and `execute_note_add` to
      `crates/rivets/src/cli/execute.rs`: fetch issue (error if missing), build entry via
      `build_timestamped_note(Utc::now(), text)`, `append_note`, then `update` with
      `IssueUpdate { notes: Some(appended), ..Default::default() }`
    - Persist via `app.save()` with reload-on-failure; emit JSON or `Added note to {id}` per output mode
    - Wire `Some(Commands::Note(args)) => ... execute::execute_note(...)` in `Cli::execute`
    - _Requirements: 1.2, 1.3, 1.4, 2.3, 4.1_

  - [ ]* 3.2 Write property test for adding a note to a missing issue
    - **Property 4: Adding a note to a missing issue is a no-op error**
    - **Validates: Requirements 4.1**
    - For any store and any absent id, assert an `IssueNotFound` error and the stored set is unchanged

  - [ ]* 3.3 Write example test for identity exclusion in a note entry
    - Add a note and assert the produced entry contains only the timestamp header and text (no author/assignee)
    - _Requirements: 2.3_

- [ ] 4. Checkpoint - Ensure all tests pass
  - Ensure all tests pass, ask the user if questions arise.

- [ ] 5. Cover overwrite semantics and backward-compatible storage
  - [ ]* 5.1 Write property test for notes serialization round-trip
    - **Property 5: Notes survive a serialization round-trip**
    - **Validates: Requirements 3.2, 3.3**
    - Include issues whose `notes` hold multiple accumulated timestamped entries; assert JSONL round-trip equality

  - [ ]* 5.2 Write property test for `update --notes` overwrite semantics
    - **Property 6: `update --notes` overwrites the entire field**
    - **Validates: Requirements 3.1**
    - For any prior notes and any replacement value, assert `Notes_Field` equals exactly the replacement

  - [ ]* 5.3 Write example test for loading a pre-feature issue
    - Deserialize a JSONL issue created before this feature and assert its `notes` field loads without error
    - _Requirements: 3.3_

- [ ] 6. Implement MCP parity
  - [ ] 6.1 Add `NoteAddParams` model
    - Add `NoteAddParams { issue_id, text, workspace_root }` (with `JsonSchema`) to
      `crates/rivets-mcp/src/models.rs`
    - _Requirements: 5.1_

  - [ ] 6.2 Implement `Tools::note_add`
    - Add `note_add` to `crates/rivets-mcp/src/tools.rs`: reject empty/whitespace-only text
      (`InvalidArgument`), resolve storage, fetch issue (error if missing), build entry via
      `rivets::domain::build_timestamped_note` + `rivets::domain::append_note`, `update`, and
      save with reload-on-failure
    - _Requirements: 5.2, 5.3_

  - [ ] 6.3 Register the `note_add` tool on the server
    - Add the `#[tool(...)]` `note_add` method to `crates/rivets-mcp/src/server.rs`, delegating to
      `Tools::note_add` and mapping errors via `to_mcp_error`
    - _Requirements: 5.1_

  - [ ]* 6.4 Write property test for CLI/MCP entry equivalence
    - **Property 7: CLI and MCP produce equivalent note entries**
    - **Validates: Requirements 5.2**
    - For any prior notes and any text (fixed instant), assert the MCP-appended result equals the CLI-appended result

  - [ ]* 6.5 Write example tests for MCP tool registration and missing-id error
    - Assert `note_add` is registered/listed on the server (5.1) and that an absent id yields a
      missing-issue error mapped through `to_mcp_error` (5.3)
    - _Requirements: 5.1, 5.3_

- [ ] 7. Final checkpoint - Ensure all tests pass
  - Ensure all tests pass, ask the user if questions arise.

## Notes

- Tasks marked with `*` are optional and can be skipped for a faster MVP.
- Each task references specific requirements for traceability; property-test sub-tasks reference
  their design property number and the requirement clauses they validate.
- Time is injected into `build_timestamped_note(now, text)` so property tests compare entries
  without racing on the wall clock.
- The append/timestamp logic lives once in `rivets::domain::notes`; both CLI and MCP compose the
  same helpers, so no note-formatting logic is duplicated.
- Checkpoints ensure incremental validation before moving on to the MCP layer.

## Task Dependency Graph

```json
{
  "waves": [
    { "id": 0, "tasks": ["1.1", "2.1", "6.1"] },
    { "id": 1, "tasks": ["1.2", "1.3", "2.2", "2.3"] },
    { "id": 2, "tasks": ["3.1", "6.2"] },
    { "id": 3, "tasks": ["2.4", "3.2", "3.3", "6.3"] },
    { "id": 4, "tasks": ["5.1", "5.2", "5.3", "6.4", "6.5"] }
  ]
}
```

# Implementation Plan: Multiple Notes

## Overview

Implement the `rivets note add <id> "text"` subcommand and its MCP counterpart, which append a
UTC-timestamped entry to an issue's `notes` field without discarding prior content. The work
starts by extracting the note-formatting logic into a shared `rivets::domain::notes` module
(`build_timestamped_note` plus the relocated `append_note`) so both the CLI and MCP paths compose
the same helpers. It then layers CLI argument parsing, input validation, and the command handler,
followed by MCP parity. Storage stays unchanged (`notes` remains `Option<String>`), keeping the
change backward compatible. Property-based tests validate the seven correctness properties from
the design; focused example/integration tests cover parsing, error paths, and backward-compatible
loading.

## Tasks

- [ ] 1. Create shared domain note-formatting module
  - [ ] 1.1 Add `rivets::domain::notes` module and relocate the append helper
    - Create `crates/rivets/src/domain/notes.rs` with `NOTE_TIMESTAMP_FORMAT` and pure
      `build_timestamped_note(now: DateTime<Utc>, text: &str) -> String` (header line + `\n` + text)

      `pub`; re-export both from `crate::domain` so CLI and MCP crates share one import
    - Update existing `close`/`reopen` call sites in `execute.rs` to use the relocated `append_note`
    - _Requirements: 1.2, 1.3, 1.4, 2.1, 2.2, 2.4_

    - **Property 2: Note entry is a UTC timestamp header line followed by the text**

    - **Validates: Requirements 4.2**
    - Generate whitespace-only strings (including empty) and assert `validate_note_text` returns an error

  - [ ] 2.3 Add `NoteArgs`/`NoteAction` and `Commands::Note` variant
    - Add `NoteArgs` and `NoteAction::Add { issue_id, text }` to `crates/rivets/src/cli/args.rs`,
      wiring `validate_issue_id` and `validate_note_text`, with `allow_hyphen_values` on `text`
    - Add the `Note(NoteArgs)` variant (with doc comment) to the `Commands` enum in
      `crates/rivets/src/cli/mod.rs`
    - _Requirements: 1.1_

  - [ ]* 2.4 Write example test for `note add` clap parsing
    - Assert `note add <id> "text"` parses into `NoteAction::Add` and that a leading `-` in text is accepted
    - _Requirements: 1.1_

- [ ] 3. Implement the CLI command handler
  - [ ] 3.1 Implement `execute_note`/`execute_note_add` and dispatch
    - Add `execute_note` (dispatches on `NoteAction`) and `execute_note_add` to
      `crates/rivets/src/cli/execute.rs`: fetch issue (error if missing), build entry via
      `build_timestamped_note(Utc::now(), text)`, `append_note`, then `update` with
      `IssueUpdate { notes: Some(appended), ..Default::default() }`
    - Persist via `app.save()` with reload-on-failure; emit JSON or `Added note to {id}` per output mode
    - Wire `Some(Commands::Note(args)) => ... execute::execute_note(...)` in `Cli::execute`
    - _Requirements: 1.2, 1.3, 1.4, 2.3, 4.1_

  - [ ]* 3.2 Write property test for adding a note to a missing issue
    - **Property 4: Adding a note to a missing issue is a no-op error**
    - **Validates: Requirements 4.1**
    - For any store and any absent id, assert an `IssueNotFound` error and the stored set is unchanged

  - [ ]* 3.3 Write example test for identity exclusion in a note entry
    - Add a note and assert the produced entry contains only the timestamp header and text (no author/assignee)
    - _Requirements: 2.3_

- [ ] 4. Checkpoint - Ensure all tests pass
  - Ensure all tests pass, ask the user if questions arise.

- [ ] 5. Cover overwrite semantics and backward-compatible storage
  - [ ]* 5.1 Write property test for notes serialization round-trip
    - **Property 5: Notes survive a serialization round-trip**
    - **Validates: Requirements 3.2, 3.3**
    - Include issues whose `notes` hold multiple accumulated timestamped entries; assert JSONL round-trip equality

  - [ ]* 5.2 Write property test for `update --notes` overwrite semantics
    - **Property 6: `update --notes` overwrites the entire field**
    - **Validates: Requirements 3.1**
    - For any prior notes and any replacement value, assert `Notes_Field` equals exactly the replacement

  - [ ]* 5.3 Write example test for loading a pre-feature issue
    - Deserialize a JSONL issue created before this feature and assert its `notes` field loads without error
    - _Requirements: 3.3_

- [ ] 6. Implement MCP parity
  - [ ] 6.1 Add `NoteAddParams` model
    - Add `NoteAddParams { issue_id, text, workspace_root }` (with `JsonSchema`) to
      `crates/rivets-mcp/src/models.rs`
    - _Requirements: 5.1_

    - Add `note_add` to `crates/rivets-mcp/src/tools.rs`: reject empty/whitespace-only text

    - _Requirements: 5.2, 5.3_

    - Add the `#[tool(...)]` `note_add` method to `crates/rivets-mcp/src/server.rs`, delegating to

    - _Requirements: 5.1_

    - **Property 7: CLI and MCP produce equivalent note entries**
    - **Validates: Requirements 5.2**
    - For any prior notes and any text (fixed instant), assert the MCP-appended result equals the CLI-appended result

  - [ ]* 6.5 Write example tests for MCP tool registration and missing-id error
    - Assert `note_add` is registered/listed on the server (5.1) and that an absent id yields a

    - _Requirements: 5.1, 5.3_

# Implementation Plan: Multiple Notes

## Overview

Implement the `rivets note add <id> "text"` subcommand and its MCP counterpart, which append a
UTC-timestamped entry to an issue's `notes` field without discarding prior content. The work
starts by extracting the note-formatting logic into a shared `rivets::domain::notes` module
(`build_timestamped_note` plus the relocated `append_note`) so both the CLI and MCP paths compose
the same helpers. It then layers CLI argument parsing, input validation, and the command handler,
followed by MCP parity. Storage stays unchanged (`notes` remains `Option<String>`), keeping the
change backward compatible. Property-based tests validate the seven correctness properties from
the design; focused example/integration tests cover parsing, error paths, and backward-compatible
loading.

## Tasks

- [ ] 1. Create shared domain note-formatting module
  - [ ] 1.1 Add `rivets::domain::notes` module and relocate the append helper
    - Create `crates/rivets/src/domain/notes.rs` with `NOTE_TIMESTAMP_FORMAT` and pure
      `build_timestamped_note(now: DateTime<Utc>, text: &str) -> String` (header line + `\n` + text)
    - Move `append_note` out of `crates/rivets/src/cli/execute.rs` into this module and make it
      `pub`; re-export both from `crate::domain` so CLI and MCP crates share one import
    - Update existing `close`/`reopen` call sites in `execute.rs` to use the relocated `append_note`
    - _Requirements: 1.2, 1.3, 1.4, 2.1, 2.2, 2.4_

  - [ ]* 1.2 Write property test for `build_timestamped_note`
    - **Property 2: Note entry is a UTC timestamp header line followed by the text**
    - **Validates: Requirements 2.1, 2.2, 2.4**
    - Assert first line matches `[YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS UTC]` and remaining content equals the input text

  - [ ]* 1.3 Write property test for `append_note` separation semantics
    - **Property 1: Append preserves prior notes and separates entries**
    - **Validates: Requirements 1.2, 1.3, 1.4**
    - Assert absent/empty prior → entry alone; non-empty prior → prior + `\n\n` + entry, prior is a prefix

- [ ] 2. Add CLI argument parsing and note-text validation
  - [ ] 2.1 Implement `validate_note_text` validator
    - Add `validate_note_text` to `crates/rivets/src/cli/validators.rs`: reject empty-after-trim,
      then delegate to existing `validate_text_field`; return the original (untrimmed) text
    - _Requirements: 4.2, 2.4_

  - [ ]* 2.2 Write property test for empty/whitespace-only note-text rejection
    - **Property 3: Empty or whitespace-only note text is rejected and leaves notes unchanged**

  - [ ] 2.3 Add `NoteArgs`/`NoteAction` and `Commands::Note` variant

    - _Requirements: 1.1_

- [ ] 3. Implement the CLI command handler
  - [ ] 3.1 Implement `execute_note`/`execute_note_add` and dispatch
    - Add `execute_note` (dispatches on `NoteAction`) and `execute_note_add` to

      `build_timestamped_note(Utc::now(), text)`, `append_note`, then `update` with
      `IssueUpdate { notes: Some(appended), ..Default::default() }`
    - Persist via `app.save()` with reload-on-failure; emit JSON or `Added note to {id}` per output mode
    - Wire `Some(Commands::Note(args)) => ... execute::execute_note(...)` in `Cli::execute`
    - _Requirements: 1.2, 1.3, 1.4, 2.3, 4.1_

  - [ ]* 3.2 Write property test for adding a note to a missing issue
    - **Property 4: Adding a note to a missing issue is a no-op error**
    - **Validates: Requirements 4.1**
    - For any store and any absent id, assert an `IssueNotFound` error and the stored set is unchanged

  - [ ]* 3.3 Write example test for identity exclusion in a note entry
    - Add a note and assert the produced entry contains only the timestamp header and text (no author/assignee)
    - _Requirements: 2.3_

- [ ] 4. Checkpoint - Ensure all tests pass
  - Ensure all tests pass, ask the user if questions arise.

- [ ] 5. Cover overwrite semantics and backward-compatible storage
  - [ ]* 5.1 Write property test for notes serialization round-trip
    - **Property 5: Notes survive a serialization round-trip**
    - **Validates: Requirements 3.2, 3.3**

    - _Requirements: 5.1, 5.3_

Created `.kiro/specs/multiple-notes/tasks.md` — an actionable implementation plan for the multiple-notes Quick Spec, following the design's approach.

Structure (7 top-level items, Rust, matching the project language — no language selection needed):
1. Shared domain note module — add `rivets::domain::notes` with `build_timestamped_note`, relocate `append_note` there and make it public, update close/reopen call sites (Property 1 & 2 tests).
2. CLI arg parsing + validation — `validate_note_text`, `NoteArgs`/`NoteAction::Add`, `Commands::Note` variant (Property 3 test, clap parse test).
3. CLI command handler — `execute_note`/`execute_note_add` with fetch→build→append→update→save-with-reload, plus dispatch wiring (Property 4 test, identity-exclusion example test).
4. Checkpoint.
5. Overwrite/backward-compat coverage — round-trip (Property 5), `update --notes` overwrite (Property 6), pre-feature load test.
6. MCP parity — `NoteAddParams`, `Tools::note_add`, server `#[tool]` registration (Property 7 parity test, registration + missing-id tests).
7. Final checkpoint.

All 7 correctness properties from the design are mapped to their own optional property-test sub-tasks (annotated with property number + validated requirement clauses). All test sub-tasks are marked optional with `*`; core implementation sub-tasks are not. Every requirement (1.1–5.3) is referenced by at least one task.

Included the `## Task Dependency Graph` with 5 waves. File conflicts are separated across waves (e.g. `1.1` and `3.1` both touch `execute.rs` → different waves; `6.2` tools.rs before `6.3` server.rs registration). All 17 leaf sub-tasks appear in exactly one wave; checkpoints and parent tasks are excluded.

This workflow phase produces planning artifacts only — no implementation was performed. The user can begin executing tasks by opening tasks.md and clicking "Start task" next to an item.

the same helpers. It then layers CLI argument parsing, input validation, and the command handler,
followed by MCP parity. Storage stays unchanged (`notes` remains `Option<String>`), keeping the
change backward compatible. Property-based tests validate the seven correctness properties from
the design; focused example/integration tests cover parsing, error paths, and backward-compatible
loading.

## Tasks

- [ ] 1. Create shared domain note-formatting module
  - [ ] 1.1 Add `rivets::domain::notes` module and relocate the append helper
    - Create `crates/rivets/src/domain/notes.rs` with `NOTE_TIMESTAMP_FORMAT` and pure
      `build_timestamped_note(now: DateTime<Utc>, text: &str) -> String` (header line + `\n` + text)
    - Move `append_note` out of `crates/rivets/src/cli/execute.rs` into this module and make it
      `pub`; re-export both from `crate::domain` so CLI and MCP crates share one import
    - Update existing `close`/`reopen` call sites in `execute.rs` to use the relocated `append_note`
    - _Requirements: 1.2, 1.3, 1.4, 2.1, 2.2, 2.4_

  - [ ]* 1.2 Write property test for `build_timestamped_note`
    - **Property 2: Note entry is a UTC timestamp header line followed by the text**
    - **Validates: Requirements 2.1, 2.2, 2.4**
    - Assert first line matches `[YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS UTC]` and remaining content equals the input text

  - [ ]* 1.3 Write property test for `append_note` separation semantics
    - **Property 1: Append preserves prior notes and separates entries**
    - **Validates: Requirements 1.2, 1.3, 1.4**
    - Assert absent/empty prior → entry alone; non-empty prior → prior + `\n\n` + entry, prior is a prefix

- [ ] 2. Add CLI argument parsing and note-text validation
  - [ ] 2.1 Implement `validate_note_text` validator
    - Add `validate_note_text` to `crates/rivets/src/cli/validators.rs`: reject empty-after-trim,
      then delegate to existing `validate_text_field`; return the original (untrimmed) text
    - _Requirements: 4.2, 2.4_

  - [ ]* 2.2 Write property test for empty/whitespace-only note-text rejection
    - **Property 3: Empty or whitespace-only note text is rejected and leaves notes unchanged**
    - **Validates: Requirements 4.2**
    - Generate whitespace-only strings (including empty) and assert `validate_note_text` returns an error

  - [ ] 2.3 Add `NoteArgs`/`NoteAction` and `Commands::Note` variant
    - Add `NoteArgs` and `NoteAction::Add { issue_id, text }` to `crates/rivets/src/cli/args.rs`,
      wiring `validate_issue_id` and `validate_note_text`, with `allow_hyphen_values` on `text`
    - Add the `Note(NoteArgs)` variant (with doc comment) to the `Commands` enum in
      `crates/rivets/src/cli/mod.rs`
    - _Requirements: 1.1_

  - [ ]* 2.4 Write example test for `note add` clap parsing
    - Assert `note add <id> "text"` parses into `NoteAction::Add` and that a leading `-` in text is accepted
    - _Requirements: 1.1_

- [ ] 3. Implement the CLI command handler
  - [ ] 3.1 Implement `execute_note`/`execute_note_add` and dispatch
    - Add `execute_note` (dispatches on `NoteAction`) and `execute_note_add` to
      `crates/rivets/src/cli/execute.rs`: fetch issue (error if missing), build entry via
      `build_timestamped_note(Utc::now(), text)`, `append_note`, then `update` with
      `IssueUpdate { notes: Some(appended), ..Default::default() }`
    - Persist via `app.save()` with reload-on-failure; emit JSON or `Added note to {id}` per output mode
    - Wire `Some(Commands::Note(args)) => ... execute::execute_note(...)` in `Cli::execute`
    - _Requirements: 1.2, 1.3, 1.4, 2.3, 4.1_

  - [ ]* 3.2 Write property test for adding a note to a missing issue
    - **Property 4: Adding a note to a missing issue is a no-op error**
    - **Validates: Requirements 4.1**
    - For any store and any absent id, assert an `IssueNotFound` error and the stored set is unchanged

  - [ ]* 3.3 Write example test for identity exclusion in a note entry
    - Add a note and assert the produced entry contains only the timestamp header and text (no author/assignee)
    - _Requirements: 2.3_

- [ ] 4. Checkpoint - Ensure all tests pass
  - Ensure all tests pass, ask the user if questions arise.

- [ ] 5. Cover overwrite semantics and backward-compatible storage
  - [ ]* 5.1 Write property test for notes serialization round-trip
    - **Property 5: Notes survive a serialization round-trip**
    - **Validates: Requirements 3.2, 3.3**
    - Include issues whose `notes` hold multiple accumulated timestamped entries; assert JSONL round-trip equality

  - [ ]* 5.2 Write property test for `update --notes` overwrite semantics
    - **Property 6: `update --notes` overwrites the entire field**
    - **Validates: Requirements 3.1**
    - For any prior notes and any replacement value, assert `Notes_Field` equals exactly the replacement

  - [ ]* 5.3 Write example test for loading a pre-feature issue
    - Deserialize a JSONL issue created before this feature and assert its `notes` field loads without error
    - _Requirements: 3.3_

- [ ] 6. Implement MCP parity
  - [ ] 6.1 Add `NoteAddParams` model
    - Add `NoteAddParams { issue_id, text, workspace_root }` (with `JsonSchema`) to
      `crates/rivets-mcp/src/models.rs`
    - _Requirements: 5.1_

  - [ ] 6.2 Implement `Tools::note_add`
    - Add `note_add` to `crates/rivets-mcp/src/tools.rs`: reject empty/whitespace-only text
      (`InvalidArgument`), resolve storage, fetch issue (error if missing), build entry via
      `rivets::domain::build_timestamped_note` + `rivets::domain::append_note`, `update`, and
      save with reload-on-failure
    - _Requirements: 5.2, 5.3_

  - [ ] 6.3 Register the `note_add` tool on the server
    - Add the `#[tool(...)]` `note_add` method to `crates/rivets-mcp/src/server.rs`, delegating to
      `Tools::note_add` and mapping errors via `to_mcp_error`
    - _Requirements: 5.1_

  - [ ]* 6.4 Write property test for CLI/MCP entry equivalence
    - **Property 7: CLI and MCP produce equivalent note entries**
    - **Validates: Requirements 5.2**
    - For any prior notes and any text (fixed instant), assert the MCP-appended result equals the CLI-appended result

  - [ ]* 6.5 Write example tests for MCP tool registration and missing-id error
    - Assert `note_add` is registered/listed on the server (5.1) and that an absent id yields a
      missing-issue error mapped through `to_mcp_error` (5.3)
    - _Requirements: 5.1, 5.3_

- [ ] 7. Final checkpoint - Ensure all tests pass
  - Ensure all tests pass, ask the user if questions arise.

## Notes

- Tasks marked with `*` are optional and can be skipped for a faster MVP.
- Each task references specific requirements for traceability; property-test sub-tasks reference
  their design property number and the requirement clauses they validate.
- Time is injected into `build_timestamped_note(now, text)` so property tests compare entries
  without racing on the wall clock.
- The append/timestamp logic lives once in `rivets::domain::notes`; both CLI and MCP compose the
  same helpers, so no note-formatting logic is duplicated.
- Checkpoints ensure incremental validation before moving on to the MCP layer.

## Task Dependency Graph

```json
{
  "waves": [
    { "id": 0, "tasks": ["1.1", "2.1", "6.1"] },
    { "id": 1, "tasks": ["1.2", "1.3", "2.2", "2.3"] },
    { "id": 2, "tasks": ["3.1", "6.2"] },
    { "id": 3, "tasks": ["2.4", "3.2", "3.3", "6.3"] },
    { "id": 4, "tasks": ["5.1", "5.2", "5.3", "6.4", "6.5"] }
  ]
}
```
"}

I reviewed the "multiple-notes" Quick Spec task plan at `.kiro/specs/multiple-notes/tasks.md`. Here is the implementation plan summary:

**Feature:** Add a `rivets note add <id> "text"` CLI subcommand (plus MCP equivalent) that appends a UTC-timestamped entry to an issue's `notes` field without overwriting prior content. Storage stays backward compatible (`notes` remains `Option<String>`).

**Top-level tasks:**

1. **Create shared domain note-formatting module** — Add `rivets::domain::notes` with a pure `build_timestamped_note` helper, relocate `append_note` into it, and update existing close/reopen call sites to use it.

2. **Add CLI argument parsing and note-text validation** — Implement `validate_note_text` (rejects empty/whitespace-only) and add the `NoteArgs`/`NoteAction::Add` types plus the `Commands::Note` variant.

3. **Implement the CLI command handler** — Add `execute_note`/`execute_note_add` to fetch the issue, build and append the timestamped entry, persist with reload-on-failure, and wire up dispatch.

4. **Checkpoint** — Ensure all tests pass before moving to the MCP layer.

5. **Cover overwrite semantics and backward-compatible storage** — Property/example tests for notes serialization round-trips, `update --notes` full-overwrite behavior, and loading pre-feature issues.

6. **Implement MCP parity** — Add `NoteAddParams` model, implement `Tools::note_add`, and register the `note_add` tool on the MCP server so it produces entries equivalent to the CLI.

7. **Final checkpoint** — Ensure all tests pass.

**Notes on the plan:** Tasks marked `*` are optional property/example tests that can be skipped for a faster MVP. Time is injected into `build_timestamped_note(now, text)` so tests don't race the wall clock, and the append/timestamp logic lives in one shared module used by both CLI and MCP. A task dependency graph with 5 execution waves is included for parallelization.

**Feature:** Add a `rivets note add <id> "text"` CLI subcommand (plus MCP equivalent) that appends a UTC-timestamped entry to an issue's `notes` field without overwriting prior content. Storage stays backward compatible (`notes` remains `Option<String>`).

**Top-level tasks:**

4. **Checkpoint** — Ensure all tests pass before moving to the MCP layer.

5. **Cover overwrite semantics and backward-compatible storage** — Property/example tests for notes serialization round-trips, `update --notes` full-overwrite behavior, and loading pre-feature issues.

6. **Implement MCP parity** — Add `NoteAddParams` model, implement `Tools::note_add`, and register the `note_add` tool on the MCP server so it produces entries equivalent to the CLI.

7. **Final checkpoint** — Ensure all tests pass.

**Notes on the plan:** Tasks marked `*` are optional property/example tests that can be skipped for a faster MVP. Time is injected into `build_timestamped_note(now, text)` so tests don't race the wall clock, and the append/timestamp logic lives in one shared module used by both CLI and MCP. A task dependency graph with 5 execution waves is included for parallelization.

rawOutput.content:
# Implementation Plan: Multiple Notes

## Overview

Implement the `rivets note add <id> "text"` subcommand and its MCP counterpart, which append a
UTC-timestamped entry to an issue's `notes` field without discarding prior content. The work
starts by extracting the note-formatting logic into a shared `rivets::domain::notes` module
(`build_timestamped_note` plus the relocated `append_note`) so both the CLI and MCP paths compose
the same helpers. It then layers CLI argument parsing, input validation, and the command handler,
followed by MCP parity. Storage stays unchanged (`notes` remains `Option<String>`), keeping the
change backward compatible. Property-based tests validate the seven correctness properties from
the design; focused example/integration tests cover parsing, error paths, and backward-compatible
loading.

## Tasks

- [ ] 1. Create shared domain note-formatting module
  - [ ] 1.1 Add `rivets::domain::notes` module and relocate the append helper
    - Create `crates/rivets/src/domain/notes.rs` with `NOTE_TIMESTAMP_FORMAT` and pure
      `build_timestamped_note(now: DateTime<Utc>, text: &str) -> String` (header line + `\n` + text)
    - Move `append_note` out of `crates/rivets/src/cli/execute.rs` into this module and make it
      `pub`; re-export both from `crate::domain` so CLI and MCP crates share one import
    - Update existing `close`/`reopen` call sites in `execute.rs` to use the relocated `append_note`
    - _Requirements: 1.2, 1.3, 1.4, 2.1, 2.2, 2.4_

  - [ ]* 1.2 Write property test for `build_timestamped_note`
    - **Property 2: Note entry is a UTC timestamp header line followed by the text**
    - **Validates: Requirements 2.1, 2.2, 2.4**
    - Assert first line matches `[YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS UTC]` and remaining content equals the input text

  - [ ]* 1.3 Write property test for `append_note` separation semantics
    - **Property 1: Append preserves prior notes and separates entries**
    - **Validates: Requirements 1.2, 1.3, 1.4**
    - Assert absent/empty prior → entry alone; non-empty prior → prior + `\n\n` + entry, prior is a prefix

- [ ] 2. Add CLI argument parsing and note-text validation
  - [ ] 2.1 Implement `validate_note_text` validator
    - Add `validate_note_text` to `crates/rivets/src/cli/validators.rs`: reject empty-after-trim,
      then delegate to existing `validate_text_field`; return the original (untrimmed) text
    - _Requirements: 4.2, 2.4_

  - [ ]* 2.2 Write property test for empty/whitespace-only note-text rejection
    - **Property 3: Empty or whitespace-only note text is rejected and leaves notes unchanged**
    - **Validates: Requirements 4.2**
    - Generate whitespace-only strings (including empty) and assert `validate_note_text` returns an error

  - [ ] 2.3 Add `NoteArgs`/`NoteAction` and `Commands::Note` variant
    - Add `NoteArgs` and `NoteAction::Add { issue_id, text }` to `crates/rivets/src/cli/args.rs`,
      wiring `validate_issue_id` and `validate_note_text`, with `allow_hyphen_values` on `text`
    - Add the `Note(NoteArgs)` variant (with doc comment) to the `Commands` enum in
      `crates/rivets/src/cli/mod.rs`
    - _Requirements: 1.1_

  - [ ]* 2.4 Write example test for `note add` clap parsing
    - Assert `note add <id> "text"` parses into `NoteAction::Add` and that a leading `-` in text is accepted
    - _Requirements: 1.1_

- [ ] 3. Implement the CLI command handler
  - [ ] 3.1 Implement `execute_note`/`execute_note_add` and dispatch
    - Add `execute_note` (dispatches on `NoteAction`) and `execute_note_add` to
      `crates/rivets/src/cli/execute.rs`: fetch issue (error if missing), build entry via
      `build_timestamped_note(Utc::now(), text)`, `append_note`, then `update` with
      `IssueUpdate { notes: Some(appended), ..Default::default() }`
    - Persist via `app.save()` with reload-on-failure; emit JSON or `Added note to {id}` per output mode
    - Wire `Some(Commands::Note(args)) => ... execute::execute_note(...)` in `Cli::execute`
    - _Requirements: 1.2, 1.3, 1.4, 2.3, 4.1_

  - [ ]* 3.2 Write property test for adding a note to a missing issue
    - **Property 4: Adding a note to a missing issue is a no-op error**
    - **Validates: Requirements 4.1**
    - For any store and any absent id, assert an `IssueNotFound` error and the stored set is unchanged

  - [ ]* 3.3 Write example test for identity exclusion in a note entry
    - Add a note and assert the produced entry contains only the timestamp header and text (no author/assignee)
    - _Requirements: 2.3_

- [ ] 4. Checkpoint - Ensure all tests pass
  - Ensure all tests pass, ask the user if questions arise.

- [ ] 5. Cover overwrite semantics and backward-compatible storage
  - [ ]* 5.1 Write property test for notes serialization round-trip
    - **Property 5: Notes survive a serialization round-trip**
    - **Validates: Requirements 3.2, 3.3**
    - Include issues whose `notes` hold multiple accumulated timestamped entries; assert JSONL round-trip equality

  - [ ]* 5.2 Write property test for `update --notes` overwrite semantics
    - **Property 6: `update --notes` overwrites the entire field**
    - **Validates: Requirements 3.1**
    - For any prior notes and any replacement value, assert `Notes_Field` equals exactly the replacement

  - [ ]* 5.3 Write example test for loading a pre-feature issue
    - Deserialize a JSONL issue created before this feature and assert its `notes` field loads without error
    - _Requirements: 3.3_

- [ ] 6. Implement MCP parity
  - [ ] 6.1 Add `NoteAddParams` model
    - Add `NoteAddParams { issue_id, text, workspace_root }` (with `JsonSchema`) to
      `crates/rivets-mcp/src/models.rs`
    - _Requirements: 5.1_

  - [ ] 6.2 Implement `Tools::note_add`
    - Add `note_add` to `crates/rivets-mcp/src/tools.rs`: reject empty/whitespace-only text
      (`InvalidArgument`), resolve storage, fetch issue (error if missing), build entry via
      `rivets::domain::build_timestamped_note` + `rivets::domain::append_note`, `update`, and
      save with reload-on-failure
    - _Requirements: 5.2, 5.3_

  - [ ] 6.3 Register the `note_add` tool on the server
    - Add the `#[tool(...)]` `note_add` method to `crates/rivets-mcp/src/server.rs`, delegating to
      `Tools::note_add` and mapping errors via `to_mcp_error`
    - _Requirements: 5.1_

  - [ ]* 6.4 Write property test for CLI/MCP entry equivalence
    - **Property 7: CLI and MCP produce equivalent note entries**
    - **Validates: Requirements 5.2**
    - For any prior notes and any text (fixed instant), assert the MCP-appended result equals the CLI-appended result

  - [ ]* 6.5 Write example tests for MCP tool registration and missing-id error
    - Assert `note_add` is registered/listed on the server (5.1) and that an absent id yields a
      missing-issue error mapped through `to_mcp_error` (5.3)
    - _Requirements: 5.1, 5.3_

- [ ] 7. Final checkpoint - Ensure all tests pass
  - Ensure all tests pass, ask the user if questions arise.

## Notes

- Tasks marked with `*` are optional and can be skipped for a faster MVP.
- Each task references specific requirements for traceability; property-test sub-tasks reference
  their design property number and the requirement clauses they validate.
- Time is injected into `build_timestamped_note(now, text)` so property tests compare entries
  without racing on the wall clock.
- The append/timestamp logic lives once in `rivets::domain::notes`; both CLI and MCP compose the
  same helpers, so no note-formatting logic is duplicated.
- Checkpoints ensure incremental validation before moving on to the MCP layer.

## Task Dependency Graph

```json
{
  "waves": [
    { "id": 0, "tasks": ["1.1", "2.1", "6.1"] },
    { "id": 1, "tasks": ["1.2", "1.3", "2.2", "2.3"] },
    { "id": 2, "tasks": ["3.1", "6.2"] },
    { "id": 3, "tasks": ["2.4", "3.2", "3.3", "6.3"] },
    { "id": 4, "tasks": ["5.1", "5.2", "5.3", "6.4", "6.5"] }
  ]
}
```
